title: Level 991
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 991:''' "The Grid of Bridges"}}
'''A little notice (Do not remove!):''' About the deletion template that was added before the level was sandboxed, the issues are all fixed. [[User:PlutoniumOnInternet|Plutonium]], the one who added the template, again, it has been fixed. Thanks for the little support on the bottom of the template! If this still isn't good, message wall me, ping me on this page, or message me on Discord.
---- ---- ---- ---- [[File:964 Poster.png|thumb|center|800px]] ---- ---- ---- ----
'''Level 991'''{{Underline|FFFFFF}}{{Underline|FFFFFF}}

When I was younger, I loved adventure activities and climbing. But this? '''Hell no.''' I can't see anything, there's cobwebs everywhere, and I feel like something is watching me… {{Image Banner|image=Level 964 1.jpg|caption=''As I walked through the bridges of '''Level 991''', I felt the cobwebs all over my face, even inside my mouth…''}} ---- {{Alt Class|no=991|ext=2|env=2|ent=4}} ---- ---- '''Level 991''' is the '''992nd''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. ---- ---- =‎ '''> Description'''= ---- ----
{{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|box-shadow-color=white|align=right|file=Level 964 1.jpg|caption=Image from the North Side of Grid Square 771. An entity's lanky hand can be seen hidden in the dark.|hovertext=WATCH OUT|𝚏𝚒𝚐=1.0}} '''Level 991''' resembles a 3,500 × 3,500-mile grid of bridges, with each grid square varied in size from 1 x 1 mile to 30 x 30 miles at its largest. These squares have 35-foot-wide bridges around the edges and a gap in the center. Located 1000 meters above the forest floor, one might see unusually tall trees reaching heights of about 4,500 to 5,200 meters. '''[[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]''' can rarely be found on the east and west sides of grid squares, with cables leading from the bridge to the forest floor. Occasionally, full World War II military tents can be found in damaged or perfect condition in various squares, all holding World War II military equipment such as Mauser Karabiner 98ks, MP40 Submachine Guns, and regular World War II & I German military outfits. It is advised to be cautious near these tents, as entities have been known to occupy them. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|box-shadow-color=white|align=right|file=Level 964.jpg|caption=Image from the east side of Grid Square 338.|hovertext=WATCH OUT|𝚏𝚒𝚐=2.0}} Supplies are made and moved between these tents, presumably by a group of soldiers. Due to the ongoing rain and moisture, bridges can become extremely damp, weakening them and causing them to collapse. It is recommended to pay attention to the structure of the bridges in order to avoid standing on collapsing bridges. Bridges often have lamps with cables running down the centers of grid squares. It is best to stay in these illuminated areas at night, as entities usually can be found in darker areas. Entities can still go to the lights, but this is a 10% chance of happening. As the sky gets darker, the lamps begin to flicker in random patterns, switching off or dimming for 4 to 16 seconds before turning back on. When this happens, loud and echoing footsteps can be heard far away under the forest canopy. The footsteps are too loud and sound too abnormal for mammals and other [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] fauna. Wanderers have reported to '''[[M.E.G.]]''' or a colony/outpost in '''Level 991''', hearing metal clanging from beneath the bridges when footsteps can be heard, and the lamp starts flickering, as if something has been messing with some abandoned electricity or waste under the bridges. '''''‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ …What was that??'''''

---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Bunkers'''=== ---- ---- {{Alt Class|no=991|ext=0|env=0|ent=1}} ---- ----
{{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|box-shadow-color=white|align=right|file=Dülmen, Kirchspiel, ehem. Munitionslager Visbeck, Bunker -- 2019 -- 6432 (bw).png|caption=A photograph of one of the rooms in '''The Bunkers''' taken by an unknown wanderer.|hovertext=THE BUNKER|𝚏𝚒𝚐=3.0}} '''The Bunkers''' are a large complex sometimes compared to WWII bunkers from the 1940s. The structure of '''The Bunkers''' is dirty, and the paint on the walls appears to be fading over time. '''The Bunkers''' are an infinite series of connected rooms that can be entered by finding a tree and a door. Entering this door will take you to '''The Bunkers'''. A room inside '''The Bunkers''' is mostly empty; some rooms are decorated with furniture or wallpaper that can represent different levels. Non-empty rooms within '''The Bunkers''' include colonies and outposts. Depending on the level, these rooms may have furnishings. If the level is empty (e.g., '''[[Level 296]]'''), the room will include only wallpaper and flooring. When entering '''The Bunkers,''' each bunker/room will be identified by a number. The bunker/room where one starts will be marked as 0. The negatives will be in the left (-1, -2, -3, -4, -5, -6, -7, etc.). Positive numbers (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, etc.) will be in the right direction. The bunkers/rooms are connected via glass doors. The lower the number, the colder it gets; the higher the number, the hotter it gets.
---- ---- =‎ '''> Entities'''= ---- ----
Various entities can be found in the bridges of '''Level 991''' such as '''[[Entity 3|Smilers]]''', '''Soldiers''', '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]''', '''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]''' and '''[[Entity 6|Dullers]]'''. Massive spiders from '''[[Level 8]]''' can also be found in '''Level 991'''. A level exclusive entity found in the bridges of '''Level 991''' is called '''The Lanky Body'''. More information about it can be found below:
---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Lanky Body'''=== ---- ----
{{Entity Class|Agg=4|Int=5|Dan=5|no=N/A|Hab=Only [[Level 991]]}} '''The Lanky Body''' is a tall humanoid being that is found on '''Level 991's''' bridges, especially around damaged areas or deactivated lamps. Its body appears very thin and long, about between 3 and 6 meters in height. The height can be inconsistent depending on the way you move your head. Its arms are lanky and long, which sometimes the arms can extend over the edges of bridges into the fog below. The hand can reach humans, no matter how far away the wanderer/hand is. When the hand grabs a wanderer, the hand will lift the wanderer up to its head, pulling the wanderer into its mouth, eating the wanderer alive. '''The Lanky Body''' cannot digest any food/wanderers, so, '''The Lanky Body''' might combust if it eats too much. When '''The Lanky Body''' combusts, you will be sent to any anomalous level like '''[[The Broken]]''', '''[[The Core]]''' or '''[[Level REDACTED]]'''. '''The Lanky Body''' has no visible face. Where a head should be, there is a tiny dent or fold that occasionally turns his face but never shows its features. Despite this, it has eye sight and can hear.
---- ---- =‎ '''> Colonies & Outposts'''= ---- ----
‎A total of 13 human outposts and 2 colonies can be found on '''Level 991''', making it easy to communicate and find. Maps have been created of the entire grid and placed throughout the level. Most of these colonies & outposts reside in '''The Bunkers''' as the bridges are way too unstable and dangerous to make a colony or an outpost. ‎Only 5 colonies/outposts have been documented and discovered by '''[[M.E.G.]]''' and other groups, as the other 10 colonies/outposts have been found burning bodies, most likely from wanderers in '''[[M.E.G.]]''' and other groups. ‎Due to this, most wanderers are too afraid to enter those colonies/outposts. The 5 colonies/outposts you see have been documented with permission from them. The burnt wanderer's bodies were killed and burnt in a fire, which was then turned into meat that the colonies/outposts would eat. Audio has been found of those wanderers asking the colonies/outposts to gain permission to mention them in the document. ‎The audio has been kept in a secret vault by '''[[M.E.G.]]''' and is protected by friendly '''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]''', which pull out deadly weapons/bombs when attacked or finding someone/something attempting to steal the locked-away audio. The hidden passcode to the vault is ██████. ---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Dominion of 991'''=== ---- ---- *A group made of 85 members, inhabiting 3 military tents in squares 43, 56, and 87. *Led by a 5-person shadow council and 1 Emperor, currently '''"Wilhelm II."''' *Heavily armed, looting World War 2 equipment from tents. '''The Soldiers''' are recognized by wearing World War II German outfits. *Founded in 1987 by a man calling himself '''"Wilhelm."''' *Engaged in conflict with the '''Skin-Stealer Pack'''. ---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Mapping Guild'''=== ---- ---- *A group made up of 40 people. *A guild spanning '''The Dominion of 991''' and '''The Federation of 991'''. *Dedicated to mapping out the grid of '''Level 991''', tents, light, and dark zones. *Has 3 mapping outposts scattered around the grid, often abandoned and only used in case of new outposts. ---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Federation of 991'''=== ---- ---- *A group of 60 people that has set up very few trade outposts on bare bridges, but lots of outposts in the bunkers. *2 trade outposts on bridges throughout the grid, and 1 main base located in a tent on '''grid square 176'''. Another main base can be found in the bunkers. *Led by an elected president. *Leads an effort to stop dark areas, sending military operations to dark bridges and placing light sources. *Engaged in conflict with the '''Skin-Stealer Pack'''. ---- ---- ===‎ '''> The [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealer]] Pack'''=== ---- ---- *A strangely united group of 150 soldiers as an alternative form of '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]'''. *Due to the strange behaviors of these '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]''', the '''Federation Research Organization''' declared them as their own entity known as '''Soldiers'''. *This pack inhabits the majority of the tents in '''Level 991'''. *Engaged in military conflict with the '''Federation of 991''' and the '''Dominion of 991'''. ---- ---- ===‎ '''> The Federation Research Organization'''=== ---- ---- *A group of 14 researchers working for '''The Federation Research Organization'''. *Actively researching the strange behavior of '''The [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealer]] Pack'''. *Developing technology to find out what is causing the strange lightning behavior and discovering what is in the center of each square. *Investigating the history behind '''Level 991'''.
---- ---- =‎ '''> Entrances & Exits'''= ---- ---- ===‎ '''> Entrances'''‎=== ---- ----
*A German Iron Cross can be found via '''The Main Nine''' (except '''[[Level 8]]'''), and when touched has a chance to noclip the wanderer into '''Level 991'''. *Some catwalks in '''[[Level 6]]''' or '''[[Level 190]]''' gradually shift to wooden bridges similar to different levels, resulting in a noclip to '''Level 991'''. *Although unconfirmed, it is said that a tunnel in '''[[Level 8]]''' or '''[[Level 990]]''' leads to the forest in '''Level 991'''. However, no tunnel of this type has been discovered.
---- ---- ===‎‎ '''> Exits'''‎=== ---- ----
*All bridges at the edges of the grid blast off and wander towards the edge of the grid, causing you to noclip into '''[[Level 6]]'''. *On the east bridge of '''Square 507''', the bridge will appear to look like the yellow and wet carpet from '''[[Level 0]]'''. Lying down on the carpet for long periods of time will make one fall through the floor, sending them into '''[[Level 0]]''' or '''[[Level List|yellow-like levels]]'''.
